prompt: Using ProteinMPNN-guided ancestral sequence reconstruction, infer ancestral influenza A PB2 sequences across the avian-to-human host adaptation trajectory using a curated alignment of 800 PB2 sequences spanning H5N1, H7N9, H3N2, and H1N1 subtypes from NCBI Influenza Virus Resource. Generate ProteinMPNN sequences for ancestral PB2 cap-binding domain backbones at reconstructed zoonotic host-switch nodes. outline a methodology covering: (1) subtype-stratified multiple sequence alignment and maximum-likelihood phylogeny, (2) ancestral state reconstruction with FastML for cap-binding domain positions, (3) ESMFold backbone generation and ProteinMPNN sampling, (4) scoring by predicted mammalian vs. avian binding free energy difference. Discuss how ancestral reconstruction can inform pandemic risk assessment for influenza A.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and NCBI Influenza Virus Resource; target: PB2 cap-binding domain; organism: Influenza A virus; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: cap-binding subunit of RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (host adaptation determinant)
